task_instruction:
Compare the draft credit agreement against the commitment letter, term sheet, and no-flex confirmation, and flag all deviations in a structured report with an executive summary memo.

Output: `deviation-report.xlsx` and `executive-summary.docx`.

key_entities: Hawthorne Capital Partners Fund VI, LP

entries.content: This is a comparison and issue-flagging task supported by targeted extraction. The approach involves extracting the baseline terms from the term sheet, commitment letter, and no-flex confirmation, extracting the corresponding drafted terms from the draft credit agreement, and performing a side-by-side gap analysis to identify any deviations, unauthorized changes, or missing provisions. The analysis must specifically verify if any 'flex' terms were improperly incorporated despite the 'no-flex' confirmation.
COMPLETENESS CRITERION: A completed deviation-report.xlsx that addresses all 20 items listed in the comparison template.
COMPLETENESS CRITERION: For each of the 20 items, the report must show the term sheet/commitment letter baseline, the draft credit agreement term, and a clear 'Match/Deviation' flag.
COMPLETENESS CRITERION: An executive summary memo (executive-summary.docx) summarizing the key findings, material deviations, and commercial impact.
COMPLETENESS CRITERION: Specific confirmation of whether the draft credit agreement complies with the 'no-flex' confirmation.
COMPLETENESS CRITERION: Identification of any unauthorized fees or structural changes not contemplated in the commitment documents.
COMPLETENESS CRITERION: Clear recommendations for the negotiation team on which deviations must be contested.
Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC commits to provide a first lien senior secured term loan B facility in an aggregate principal amount of $350,000,000.
Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC commits to provide a first lien senior secured revolving credit facility in an aggregate principal amount of $75,000,000.
The Borrower (HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC) is required to provide a minimum equity contribution from Hawthorne Capital Partners Fund VI, LP of not less than $330,000,000.
The Commitment Letter and commitments expire at 5:00 p.m. (Eastern Time) on September 15, 2025, if the closing has not occurred.
The aggregate enterprise value of the Target (Ridgeline Manufacturing Group, Inc.) is $780,000,000, with an equity purchase price of $720,000,000.
The Commitment Letter and Fee Letter must be accepted by 5:00 p.m. (Eastern Time) on June 5, 2025.
Borrower is HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (EIN: 85-4023198).
Term Loan B principal amount is $350,000,000.
Revolving Credit Facility commitment amount is $75,000,000.
Term Loan B interest rate is Adjusted Term SOFR plus 400 basis points (4.00%) per annum with a SOFR floor of 0.50%.
Term Loan B OID is 2.00% ($7,000,000) issued at 98.0% of par.
Term Loan B maturity is seven (7) years from the Closing Date (anticipated July 31, 2032).
Term Loan B amortization is 1.00% per annum ($3,500,000 per annum or $875,000 per quarter).
Soft Call Premium on Term Loan B is 1.00% for any voluntary prepayment or repricing within six (6) months after the Closing Date.
Excess Cash Flow Sweep: 50% if First Lien Net Leverage Ratio > 3.75 to 1.00; 25% if > 3.25 to 1.00 but <= 3.75 to 1.00; 0% if <= 3.25 to 1.00.
Asset Sale mandatory prepayment threshold is $7,500,000 per annum.
Asset Sale reinvestment period is 365 days, extendable by 180 days if contractually committed (total 545 days).
Springing First Lien Net Leverage Ratio covenant is 6.25 to 1.00, tested if Revolver utilization exceeds 35% ($26,250,000).
Equity Cure rights limited to 2 times in any 4 consecutive fiscal quarters and 5 times during the term.
General Basket for Restricted Payments is the greater of $15,000,000 and 15% of Consolidated EBITDA.
Arrangement Fee: 1.75% of the aggregate principal amount of the Term Loan B ($350,000,000), totaling $6,125,000, payable to Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC on the Closing Date.
Upfront Fee (OID): 2.00% of the aggregate principal amount of the Term Loan B ($350,000,000), totaling $7,000,000, resulting in a price of 98.0.
Administrative Agent Fee: $150,000 per annum, payable to Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC annually in advance commencing on the Closing Date.
Commitment Fee: 0.375% per annum on the average daily undrawn portion of the Revolving Facility, payable quarterly in arrears.
Ticking Fee: 0.25% per annum on the aggregate undrawn commitments ($425,000,000) if the Closing Date has not occurred by July 21, 2025 (60 days after May 22, 2025).
Interest Rate Margin Flex: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC may increase the Term Loan B margin by up to 50 basis points and the Revolving Facility margin by up to 25 basis points.
OID Flex: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC may increase the original issue discount on the Term Loan B by up to 1.00% (to a floor price of 97.0).
SOFR Floor Flex: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC may increase the SOFR floor on the Term Loan B by up to 25 basis points (from 0.50% to 0.75%).
Structural Flex: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC may modify financial covenant levels, provided the springing covenant threshold is not reduced below 30% of the Revolving Facility commitment.
Parties: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC (Arranger/Agent) and HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC (Borrower).
Total maximum OID for Term Loan B is 3.00%.
Northbrook may increase quarterly amortization on the Term Loan B up to 1.00% per annum.
Current quarterly amortization for Term Loan B is set at 1.00% per annum as per Section 5 of Exhibit A to the Commitment Letter.
Northbrook may increase the SOFR floor on the Term Loan B by up to 25 basis points, from 0.50% to a maximum of 0.75%.
The Revolving Facility SOFR floor is not subject to flex.

Structural flex allows Northbrook to make modifications to Credit Facilities to facilitate syndication, provided the springing covenant threshold in Section 9 of Exhibit A is not reduced below 30% of the Revolving Facility commitment.
Flex Rights expire upon the earlier of (a) successful completion of syndication or (b) the Closing Date.
As of May 22, 2025, no Flex Rights have been exercised.
Permitted legal counsel for disclosure: Ashford, Kline & Pemberton LLP.
Permitted financial advisor for disclosure: Pinnacle Advisory Services, LLC.
Permitted auditor for disclosure: Aldersgate Accounting Group LLP.
Fee Letter is governed by the laws of the State of New York, specifically referencing Sections 5-1401 and 5-1402 of the New York General Obligations Law.
Exclusive jurisdiction for disputes: United States District Court for the Southern District of New York or the Supreme Court of the State of New York, New York County.
Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC is represented by David Sung, Managing Director, Leveraged Finance.
HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC is represented by Marcus Roth, Authorized Signatory.
Section 1.02(a) specifies that the meanings of defined terms are equally applicable to both the singular and plural forms of the defined terms.
Section 1.02(b) establishes that the words 'herein', 'hereof', and 'hereunder' refer to the Credit Agreement as a whole.
Section 1.02(b) establishes that references to 'Articles', 'Sections', 'Exhibits', and 'Schedules' refer to the Credit Agreement unless otherwise specified.
Section 1.02(c) mandates that the word 'including' is not limiting and shall be deemed to be followed by the phrase 'without limitation'.
Section 1.02(d) provides that the word 'or' is not exclusive.
Section 1.02(e) stipulates that references to any agreement or instrument include all supplements, amendments, and restatements thereof.
Section 1.02(f) states that references to any Person include the successors and permitted assigns of such Person.
Section 1.02(g) clarifies that references to any law include all statutory and regulatory provisions consolidating, amending, or replacing such law.
Section 1.02(h) dictates that the captions and headings are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the construction of the agreement.
Section 1.02(i) establishes that the agreement was negotiated by each party with the benefit of legal counsel.
Section 1.02(j) provides that no rule of strict construction shall be applied against any party due to the drafting of the agreement.
Section 1.02(k) specifies that references to 'knowledge' of the Borrower mean the actual knowledge of a Responsible Officer.
Section 1.02(l) mandates that all accounting terms not specifically defined shall be construed in accordance with GAAP.
Section 1.02(m) states that all financial statements delivered shall be prepared in accordance with GAAP as in effect from time to time.
Section 1.02(n) provides that if any change in GAAP occurs, the Borrower and Administrative Agent shall negotiate in good faith to amend the financial covenants.
Section 1.08 is titled 'Limited Condition Transactions'.
Article II is titled 'The Commitments and Credit Extensions'.
The provided source text for Section 1.08 contains no substantive text, definitions, or conditions beyond the header.
The provided source text for Article II contains no substantive text, definitions, or conditions beyond the header.
The source document provided is missing the body text for Section 1.08 and Article II, preventing extraction of specific interest rate margins, SOFR floors, or OID.
The source document provided is missing the body text for Section 1.08 and Article II, preventing extraction of maturity dates or amortization schedules.
The source document provided is missing the body text for Section 1.08 and Article II, preventing extraction of Revolver terms including commitment amounts or fees.
The source document provided is missing the body text for Section 1.08 and Article II, preventing extraction of Excess Cash Flow sweep percentages.
The source document provided is missing the body text for Section 1.08 and Article II, preventing extraction of Asset Sale Prepayment terms.
The source document provided is missing the body text for Section 1.08 and Article II, preventing identification of parties including Borrower, Guarantors, or Administrative Agent.
Section 2.02 is titled 'Borrowings, Conversions and Continuations'.
Borrowings of Loans of more than one Type at the same time shall be deemed separate Borrowings.
Each Borrowing shall consist entirely of Eurocurrency Rate Loans or entirely of Base Rate Loans.
The Borrower shall give the Administrative Agent irrevocable notice of each Borrowing.
Notice of Borrowing must be received by the Administrative Agent not later than 11:00 a.m. (New York City time) three Business Days before the date of the proposed Borrowing.
Notice of Borrowing for Base Rate Loans must be received by the Administrative Agent not later than 11:00 a.m. (New York City time) on the date of the proposed Borrowing.
Each notice of Borrowing shall be irrevocable and shall specify the requested date of the Borrowing.
Each notice of Borrowing shall specify the aggregate amount of such Borrowing.
Each notice of Borrowing shall specify the Type of Loans to be borrowed.
Each notice of Borrowing shall specify the initial Interest Period to be applicable thereto.
If no election as to the Type of Borrowing is specified in any such notice, the requested Borrowing shall be a Base Rate Loan.
If no Interest Period is specified in any such notice, the requested Borrowing shall have an Interest Period of one month.
The Borrower may elect to convert any Borrowing to a different Type or to continue any Borrowing as the same Type.
The Borrower shall give the Administrative Agent irrevocable notice of each conversion or continuation.

Notice of conversion or continuation must be received by the Administrative Agent not later than 11:00 a.m. (New York City time) three Business Days before the date of the proposed conversion or continuation.
Each notice of conversion or continuation shall be irrevocable and shall specify the date of such conversion or continuation.
Each notice of conversion or continuation shall specify the Borrowings to be converted or continued.
Each notice of conversion or continuation shall specify the resulting Type of Loans.
Each notice of conversion or continuation shall specify the Interest Period applicable to the resulting Loans.
If the Borrower fails to give a timely notice of conversion or continuation, the Borrowing shall be continued as a Eurocurrency Rate Loan with an Interest Period of one month.
Section 2.06 is titled 'Commitment Fee; Other Fees'.
The Borrower shall pay to the Administrative Agent for the account of each Lender a commitment fee.
The commitment fee accrues at the rate of 0.50% per annum.
The commitment fee is calculated on the daily unused amount of the Revolving Credit Commitment.
The commitment fee accrues during the period from and including the Closing Date to but excluding the date on which the Revolving Credit Commitments terminate.
Accrued commitment fees are payable in arrears on the last Business Day of each March, June, September, and December.
Accrued commitment fees are payable on the date on which the Revolving Credit Commitments terminate.
The Borrower shall pay to the Administrative Agent for the account of each Lender an upfront fee.
The upfront fee is equal to 1.00% of the aggregate principal amount of the Term Loans funded on the Closing Date.
The upfront fee is payable on the Closing Date.
The Borrower shall pay to the Administrative Agent for the account of each Lender an arrangement fee.
The arrangement fee is $250,000.
The arrangement fee is payable in two equal installments of $125,000.
The first installment of the arrangement fee is due on the Closing Date.
The second installment of the arrangement fee is due on the date that is 90 days after the Closing Date.
The Borrower shall pay to the Administrative Agent an annual agency fee of $50,000.
The annual agency fee is payable in advance on the Closing Date and on each anniversary of the Closing Date.
All fees payable under Section 2.06 are non-refundable once paid.
Fees are payable in immediately available funds.
Fees are not subject to reduction by way of set-off or counterclaim.

entries.content: Section 2.14 is titled 'Increased Costs; Capital Adequacy'.
The provision applies if any Change in Law occurs after the date of the Credit Agreement.
Change in Law includes the adoption of any law, rule, regulation, or treaty after the date of the Credit Agreement.
Change in Law includes any change in any law, rule, regulation, or treaty after the date of the Credit Agreement.
Change in Law includes any change in the interpretation or application of any law, rule, regulation, or treaty by any Governmental Authority.
Change in Law includes compliance by any Lender with any request, guideline, or directive of any Governmental Authority made after the date of the Credit Agreement.
Change in Law includes the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act.
Change in Law includes all requests, rules, guidelines, or directives under or issued in connection with the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act.

Change in Law includes all requests, rules, guidelines, or directives issued by the Bank for International Settlements, the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision, or any similar regulatory authority.
The Borrower shall pay to the Lender such additional amount or amounts as will compensate the Lender for any increased cost incurred by the Lender.
The Borrower shall pay to the Lender such additional amount or amounts as will compensate the Lender for any reduction in the amount received or receivable by the Lender.
The Borrower shall pay to the Lender such additional amount or amounts as will compensate the Lender for any reduction in the amount receivable by the Lender.
The Borrower shall pay to the Lender such additional amount or amounts as will compensate the Lender for any reduction in the rate of return on the Lender's capital.

The Borrower shall pay to the Lender such additional amount or amounts as will compensate the Lender for any reduction in the rate of return on the Lender's capital as a consequence of such Change in Law.
The Lender must deliver a certificate to the Borrower setting forth in reasonable detail the basis for the increased cost or reduction in return.
The certificate delivered by the Lender shall be conclusive absent manifest error.
The Borrower shall pay the Lender the amount shown as due on the certificate within 10 days after receipt of the certificate.
Failure or delay on the part of any Lender to demand compensation pursuant to Section 2.14 shall not constitute a waiver of the Lender's right to demand such compensation.
The Borrower shall not be required to compensate a Lender for any increased costs incurred more than 180 days prior to the date that the Lender notifies the Borrower of the Change in Law.
If the Change in Law is retroactive, the 180-day period shall be extended to include the period of retroactive effect.

The document structure skips from Section 2.17 directly to Article III.

entries.content: Section 5.01 is titled 'Existence, Qualification and Power'.
The Borrower is required to maintain its existence as a corporation, partnership, or limited liability company.
The Borrower must be duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization.
The Borrower must have all requisite power and authority to own its assets and carry on its business as now conducted.
The Borrower must be qualified to do business in all jurisdictions where such qualification is required.
Failure to be qualified in a jurisdiction is excused if the failure would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.
The Borrower must have the power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform its obligations under the Loan Documents.
The Borrower must have the power and authority to borrow under the Credit Agreement.
The Borrower must have the power and authority to grant the Liens contemplated by the Security Documents.
The execution, delivery, and performance of the Loan Documents have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate or other organizational action.
The Credit Agreement constitutes a legal, valid, and binding obligation of the Borrower.
The Credit Agreement is enforceable against the Borrower in accordance with its terms.
Enforceability is subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, or other laws affecting creditors' rights generally.
Enforceability is subject to general principles of equity, regardless of whether considered in a proceeding in equity or at law.
The Borrower's organizational documents are in full force and effect.
The Borrower is not in violation of any law, rule, or regulation in any material respect.
The Borrower is not in default with respect to any judgment, writ, injunction, or decree of any Governmental Authority in any material respect.
The Borrower possesses all requisite licenses, permits, and governmental approvals necessary to conduct its business.
The absence of such licenses or permits would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.
The Borrower is in compliance with all material terms of its material contracts.
Section 5.02 title is 'Authorization; No Contravention'.
The Borrower represents and warrants that it has the corporate power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform the Credit Agreement.
The Borrower represents and warrants that it has the corporate power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform the other Loan Documents to which it is a party.
The execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate or other organizational action.
The execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate or other organizational action.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not contravene its organizational documents.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not contravene its organizational documents.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not contravene any law applicable to the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not contravene any law applicable to the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not contravene any contractual restriction binding on the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not contravene any contractual restriction binding on the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not result in the creation of any Lien upon any property of the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not result in the creation of any Lien upon any property of the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not result in the creation of any Lien upon any property of any Subsidiary of the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not result in the creation of any Lien upon any property of any Subsidiary of the Borrower.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not result in the creation of any Lien upon any property of any Guarantor.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not result in the creation of any Lien upon any property of any Guarantor.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not violate any order of any Governmental Authority.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not violate any order of any Governmental Authority.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the Credit Agreement do not violate any order of any arbitrator.
The Borrower represents that the execution, delivery, and performance of the other Loan Documents do not violate any order of any arbitrator.
Section 5.03 is titled 'Governmental Authorization; Other Consents'.
The Borrower is required to obtain all necessary governmental authorizations.
The Borrower is required to obtain all necessary consents from third parties.
The scope of Section 5.03 covers all governmental bodies.
The scope of Section 5.03 covers all regulatory agencies.
The scope of Section 5.03 covers all other third parties.
The Borrower must ensure that all authorizations are in full force and effect.
The Borrower must ensure that all consents are in full force and effect.
The Borrower must ensure that all filings are made with governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all registrations are made with governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all required notices are provided to governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all required notices are provided to third parties.
The Borrower must ensure that all required approvals are obtained from governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all required approvals are obtained from third parties.
The Borrower must ensure that all required permits are obtained from governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all required permits are obtained from third parties.
The Borrower must ensure that all required licenses are obtained from governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all required licenses are obtained from third parties.
The Borrower must ensure that all required exemptions are obtained from governmental authorities.
The Borrower must ensure that all required exemptions are obtained from third parties.

entries.content: Section 5.08 title is Ownership of Property; Liens
The Borrower must maintain good title to all material property
The Borrower must maintain valid leasehold interests in all material property
The Borrower must maintain valid easements in all material property
The Borrower must maintain valid rights-of-way in all material property
The Borrower must maintain valid licenses in all material property
The Borrower must maintain valid permits in all material property
The Borrower must maintain valid other title defects in all material property
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Permitted Liens
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under Section 6.02
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Loan Documents
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under applicable law
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Security Documents
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Intercreditor Agreement
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Collateral Documents
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Mortgage
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Pledge Agreement
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Control Agreement
The Borrower must ensure property is free and clear of all Liens except for Liens permitted under the Intellectual Property Security Agreement
Section 5.10 title is 'Subsidiaries; Equity Interests'
The Borrower represents and warrants that Schedule 5.10 sets forth a complete and accurate list of all Subsidiaries of the Borrower as of the Closing Date
Schedule 5.10 must identify the jurisdiction of organization of each Subsidiary
Schedule 5.10 must identify the percentage of Equity Interests of each Subsidiary owned by the Borrower or any other Subsidiary
The Borrower represents that all outstanding Equity Interests of each Subsidiary are validly issued, fully paid, and non-assessable
The Borrower represents that all outstanding Equity Interests of each Subsidiary are owned free and clear of all Liens (except for Permitted Liens)

The Borrower represents that there are no outstanding subscriptions, options, warrants, calls, rights, or other agreements or commitments of any nature relating to any Equity Interests of any Subsidiary
The Borrower represents that there are no outstanding securities convertible into or exchangeable for any Equity Interests of any Subsidiary
The Borrower represents that there are no other obligations of any Subsidiary to issue, deliver, or sell, or cause to be issued, delivered, or sold, any Equity Interests of any Subsidiary
The Borrower represents that there are no obligations of any Subsidiary to repurchase, redeem, or otherwise acquire any Equity Interests of any Subsidiary
The Borrower represents that there are no voting trusts, proxies, or other agreements or understandings with respect to the voting of any Equity Interests of any Subsidiary

The Borrower represents that each Subsidiary is a corporation, partnership, limited liability company, or other entity duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization
The Borrower represents that each Subsidiary has all requisite power and authority to own its property and assets and to carry on its business as now conducted

The Borrower represents that each Subsidiary is qualified to do business in each jurisdiction where such qualification is required, except where the failure to be so qualified would not reasonably be expected to result in a Material Adverse Effect
Section 5.21 is titled 'Anti-Corruption Laws; Sanctions'.
The Borrower represents and warrants that it is in compliance with all applicable Anti-Corruption Laws.
The Borrower represents and warrants that it is in compliance with all applicable Sanctions.
The representation regarding Anti-Corruption Laws applies to the Borrower and its Subsidiaries.
The representation regarding Sanctions applies to the Borrower and its Subsidiaries.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is a Sanctioned Person.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is an agent of a Sanctioned Person.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is acting on behalf of a Sanctioned Person.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is controlled by a Sanctioned Person.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is located in a Sanctioned Country.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is organized under the laws of a Sanctioned Country.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is resident in a Sanctioned Country.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is a target of Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is the subject of Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is the subject of any investigation regarding Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is the subject of any enforcement action regarding Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is the subject of any proceeding regarding Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is the subject of any inquiry regarding Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that none of the Borrower, any Subsidiary, or any director, officer, or employee is the subject of any threat of action regarding Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that it has implemented policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with Anti-Corruption Laws.
The Borrower represents that it has implemented policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with Sanctions.
The Borrower represents that it has implemented policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with applicable Anti-Money Laundering Laws.
The Borrower represents that it has implemented policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with applicable Know Your Customer requirements.
The Borrower represents that it has implemented policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with applicable OFAC regulations.
The Borrower represents that it has implemented policies and procedures designed to ensure compliance with applicable FCPA requirements.

Article VI is titled 'Negative Covenants'.

Section 6.07 governs Transactions with Affiliates.

The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, directly or indirectly, enter into any transaction with any Affiliate of the Borrower involving aggregate payments or consideration in excess of $5,000,000.

Transactions with Affiliates must be on terms no less favorable to the Borrower or such Restricted Subsidiary than those that could be obtained in a comparable arm's-length transaction with a Person that is not an Affiliate.
Exception (a): Transactions between or among the Borrower and any Restricted Subsidiary or between or among Restricted Subsidiaries are permitted.
Exception (b): Any transaction pursuant to any contract in existence on the Closing Date and set forth on Schedule 6.07 is permitted.

Exception (c): Payment of reasonable and customary fees and compensation to, and indemnity provided on behalf of, officers, directors, employees, or consultants of the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary is permitted.
Exception (d): Transactions pursuant to the Management Services Agreement are permitted, provided such payments do not exceed $1,500,000 in any fiscal year.
Exception (e): Any issuance of Equity Interests of the Borrower to any Affiliate or any capital contribution to the Borrower is permitted.
Exception (f): Permitted Investments and Restricted Payments permitted under Section 6.04 and Section 6.06 are excluded from the restrictions of Section 6.07.
Exception (g): Transactions with a Person that is an Affiliate of the Borrower solely because the Borrower or a Restricted Subsidiary owns an Equity Interest in such Person are permitted.
Exception (h): Employment and severance arrangements between the Borrower and its officers and employees in the ordinary course of business are permitted.
Exception (i): Transactions with joint ventures or non-wholly owned subsidiaries in the ordinary course of business are permitted.
Exception (j): The payment of reasonable directors' fees and reimbursement of expenses to directors of the Borrower is permitted.
Exception (k): Any transaction with an Affiliate where the consideration is less than $500,000 is exempt from the arm's-length requirement.
Exception (l): Transactions approved by a majority of the disinterested members of the Board of Directors of the Borrower are permitted.
Exception (m): Any transaction involving the sale of inventory in the ordinary course of business to an Affiliate is permitted.
Exception (n): Any transaction involving the purchase of goods or services from an Affiliate in the ordinary course of business is permitted.
Exception (o): Any transaction involving the licensing of intellectual property on market terms is permitted.
Exception (p): Any transaction involving the provision of administrative or back-office services at cost is permitted.
Exception (q): Any transaction involving the sharing of office space or facilities at cost is permitted.
Section 6.10 is titled 'Sale and Leaseback Transactions'.
The Borrower shall not, and shall not permit any Restricted Subsidiary to, enter into any Sale and Leaseback Transaction.
The prohibition on Sale and Leaseback Transactions applies to the Borrower.
The prohibition on Sale and Leaseback Transactions applies to any Restricted Subsidiary.

Section 6.12 title is 'Fiscal Year; Accounting Changes'.
The Borrower is prohibited from changing its fiscal year without the prior written consent of the Administrative Agent.
The Borrower is prohibited from making any change in its method of accounting without the prior written consent of the Administrative Agent.
The Administrative Agent's consent for accounting changes is required unless such change is in accordance with GAAP.
The Borrower must provide notice to the Administrative Agent regarding any proposed changes to accounting methods.
The restriction on accounting changes applies to the Borrower and its Subsidiaries.
The Administrative Agent may withhold consent for fiscal year changes at its sole discretion.
The Administrative Agent may withhold consent for accounting method changes at its sole discretion.
The covenant requires the Borrower to maintain its current fiscal year end date.
The covenant requires the Borrower to maintain its current accounting principles.
Any change in accounting method must be consistent with GAAP to potentially bypass Administrative Agent consent.
The Administrative Agent is defined as Northbrook Capital Markets in the context of the credit agreement.
The Borrower is defined as HC Ridgeline Holdings in the context of the credit agreement.
Section 6.12 serves as a negative covenant restricting operational flexibility regarding financial reporting.
The requirement for Administrative Agent consent for accounting changes prevents the Borrower from unilaterally adopting new accounting standards that might impact financial covenant calculations.
Section 6.13 title: Prepayments of Subordinated Indebtedness
Borrower is prohibited from voluntarily prepaying, redeeming, purchasing, or defeasing any Subordinated Indebtedness
Prohibition applies to any payment of principal, interest, or other amounts in respect of Subordinated Indebtedness
Exception 1: Borrower may make regularly scheduled payments of principal and interest as required by the terms of the Subordinated Indebtedness
Exception 2: Borrower may make payments permitted under the subordination agreement governing the Subordinated Indebtedness
Exception 3: Borrower may make payments if such payment is expressly permitted by the terms of the subordination agreement
Restriction includes any payment made in anticipation of a scheduled payment
Restriction includes any payment made to acquire Subordinated Indebtedness for value
Restriction includes any payment made to retire Subordinated Indebtedness prior to maturity
Restriction includes any payment made to defease Subordinated Indebtedness
Restriction includes any payment made to set aside funds for the purpose of the foregoing
The term 'Subordinated Indebtedness' is subject to the definition provided in Section 1.01
The restriction applies to all direct and indirect subsidiaries of the Borrower
Any amendment to the terms of Subordinated Indebtedness that increases the principal amount is prohibited
Any amendment to the terms of Subordinated Indebtedness that increases the interest rate is prohibited
Any amendment to the terms of Subordinated Indebtedness that shortens the maturity date is prohibited
Any amendment to the terms of Subordinated Indebtedness that makes the covenants more restrictive is prohibited
Any amendment to the terms of Subordinated Indebtedness that makes the events of default more onerous is prohibited
Borrower must provide notice to the Administrative Agent prior to any permitted payment
Administrative Agent is Northbrook Capital Markets

entries.content: Section 9.01 is titled 'Appointment and Authorization'.
Each Lender irrevocably appoints Northbrook Capital Markets as the Administrative Agent.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to take such actions on behalf of the Lenders as are specifically delegated to it by the terms of the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to take such actions on behalf of the Lenders as are reasonably incidental to the actions delegated to it by the terms of the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to exercise such powers as are specifically delegated to it by the terms of the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to exercise such powers as are reasonably incidental to the powers delegated to it by the terms of the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to perform such duties as are specifically delegated to it by the terms of the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to perform such duties as are reasonably incidental to the duties delegated to it by the terms of the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the other Loan Documents.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Security Documents.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Collateral Documents.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Guarantee Documents.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Intercreditor Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Subordination Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Mortgage Documents.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Pledge Agreements.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Control Agreements.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Intellectual Property Security Agreements.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute all documents, including releases of liens, as may be required by the Financing Statements.
Section 9.04 is titled 'Reliance by Administrative Agent'.
The Administrative Agent is entitled to rely upon any notice, request, certificate, consent, statement, instrument, document or other writing believed by it to be genuine.
The Administrative Agent is entitled to rely upon any writing believed by it to be signed or presented by the proper Person or Persons.
The Administrative Agent is entitled to rely upon any statement made orally or telephone and believed by it to be made by the proper Person or Persons.

The Administrative Agent may rely upon any notice, request, certificate, consent, statement, instrument, document or other writing believed by it to be genuine and signed or presented by the proper Person or Persons.
The Administrative Agent may rely upon any statement made orally or by telephone and believed by it to be made by the proper Person or Persons.
The Administrative Agent may rely upon any statement made orally or by telephone and believed by it to be made by the proper Person or Persons, and may act upon such statement.
The Administrative Agent may rely upon any statement made orally or by telephone and believed by it to be made by the proper Person or Persons, and may act upon such statement without inquiry.

The Administrative Agent may rely upon any statement made orally or by telephone and believed by it to be made by the proper Person or Persons, and may act upon such statement without inquiry, provided that such reliance is in good faith.
The Administrative Agent may consult with legal counsel (who may be counsel for the Borrower), independent public accountants and other experts selected by it.
The Administrative Agent shall not be liable for any action taken or not taken by it in accordance with the advice of any such counsel, accountants or experts.

The Administrative Agent shall not be liable for any action taken or not taken by it in accordance with the advice of any such counsel, accountants or experts, provided such action is taken in good faith.
Section 9.06 is titled 'Resignation of Administrative Agent'.
The Administrative Agent may resign at any time by giving 30 days' prior written notice to the Lenders and the Borrower.
Upon receipt of notice of resignation, the Required Lenders shall have the right to appoint a successor Administrative Agent.

If no successor has been appointed by the Required Lenders within 30 days after the retiring Administrative Agent gives notice of resignation, the retiring Administrative Agent may appoint a successor.
The successor Administrative Agent must be a Lender or an Affiliate of a Lender.

Upon the acceptance of any appointment as Administrative Agent by a successor, such successor shall succeed to and become vested with all the rights, powers, privileges, and duties of the retiring Administrative Agent.
The retiring Administrative Agent shall be discharged from its duties and obligations under the Credit Agreement and the other Loan Documents upon the effectiveness of its resignation.

After the retiring Administrative Agent's resignation, the provisions of Article IX shall continue in effect for its benefit in respect of any actions taken or omitted to be taken by it while it was acting as Administrative Agent.
The retiring Administrative Agent is entitled to receive all fees and expenses accrued up to the date of its resignation.
The resignation of the Administrative Agent shall be effective upon the appointment of a successor Administrative Agent.

source.section: Section 9.08            Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim

entries.content: If the Administrative Agent receives payments, it shall distribute such payments to the Lenders and the Administrative Agent in accordance with the provisions of the Credit Agreement.

source.section: Section 9.08            Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim

entries.content: Nothing contained in Section 9.08 shall be deemed to authorize the Administrative Agent to authorize or consent to or accept or adopt on behalf of any Lender any plan of reorganization, arrangement, adjustment, or composition affecting the Obligations.

source.section: Section 9.08            Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim

entries.content: The Administrative Agent is prohibited from voting in respect of the claim of any Lender in any such proceeding without the consent of such Lender.

source.section: Section 9.08            Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim

entries.content: The Administrative Agent is prohibited from authorizing or consenting to any plan of reorganization, arrangement, adjustment, or composition affecting the Obligations without the consent of the Lenders.

source.section: Section 9.08            Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim

entries.content: The Administrative Agent is prohibited from authorizing or consenting to any plan of reorganization, arrangement, adjustment, or composition affecting the rights of any Lender without the consent of such Lender.

source.section: Section 9.08            Administrative Agent May File Proofs of Claim

entries.content: The Administrative Agent is prohibited from voting in respect of the claim of any Lender in any such proceeding without the express authorization of such Lender.

entries.content: Section 9.09 is titled 'Collateral and Guaranty Matters'.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to release Collateral upon the occurrence of the Termination Date.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to release Collateral upon the sale or other disposition of such Collateral in a transaction permitted under the Loan Documents.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to release Collateral if the asset is owned by a Subsidiary that ceases to be a Subsidiary as a result of a transaction permitted under the Loan Documents.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to release Collateral if the asset is owned by a Subsidiary that ceases to be a Subsidiary as a result of a transaction permitted under the Loan Documents, provided the release is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to release a Guarantor from its obligations under the Guaranty if such Person ceases to be a Subsidiary as a result of a transaction permitted under the Loan Documents.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to release a Guarantor from its obligations under the Guaranty if such Person ceases to be a Subsidiary as a result of a transaction permitted under the Loan Documents, provided the release is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to release a Guarantor from its obligations under the Guaranty upon the occurrence of the Termination Date.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to release a Guarantor from its obligations under the Guaranty if such Person is designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to release a Guarantor from its obligations under the Guaranty if such Person is designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary, provided the release is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement.
The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute and deliver all releases or other documents necessary or desirable to evidence the release of Collateral or Guarantors.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute and deliver all releases or other documents necessary or desirable to evidence the release of Collateral or Guarantors at the request of the Borrower.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute and deliver all releases or other documents necessary or desirable to evidence the release of Collateral or Guarantors at the expense of the Borrower.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute and deliver all releases or other documents necessary or desirable to evidence the release of Collateral or Guarantors without the consent of any Lender.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to execute and deliver all releases or other documents necessary or desirable to evidence the release of Collateral or Guarantors without the consent of any Lender, except as otherwise provided in the Credit Agreement.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to subordinate any Lien on any property granted to or held by the Administrative Agent under any Loan Document to the holder of any Lien on such property that is permitted by the Credit Agreement.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to subordinate any Lien on any property granted to or held by the Administrative Agent under any Loan Document to the holder of any Lien on such property that is permitted by the Credit Agreement, provided the subordination is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to subordinate any Lien on any property granted to or held by the Administrative Agent under any Loan Document to the holder of any Lien on such property that is permitted by the Credit Agreement, provided the subordination is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement, at the request of the Borrower.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to subordinate any Lien on any property granted to or held by the Administrative Agent under any Loan Document to the holder of any Lien on such property that is permitted by the Credit Agreement, provided the subordination is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement, at the expense of the Borrower.

The Administrative Agent is authorized to subordinate any Lien on any property granted to or held by the Administrative Agent under any Loan Document to the holder of any Lien on such property that is permitted by the Credit Agreement, provided the subordination is not prohibited by the Credit Agreement, without the consent of any Lender.
Section 9.10 is titled 'Indemnification of Administrative Agent'.
The Administrative Agent is entitled to indemnification under Section 9.10.
The scope of indemnification covers the Administrative Agent and its Related Parties.
Indemnification is required for any and all liabilities, obligations, losses, damages, penalties, actions, judgments, suits, costs, expenses, or disbursements of any kind or nature whatsoever.
Indemnification applies to costs incurred in connection with the execution, delivery, enforcement, performance, and administration of the Credit Agreement.
Indemnification applies to costs incurred in connection with any other document related to the Credit Agreement.
Indemnification applies to costs incurred in connection with any amendment, modification, or waiver of any provision of the Credit Agreement.
The Borrower is the primary party obligated to provide indemnification.
Indemnification is required for any claim, litigation, investigation, or proceeding relating to the Credit Agreement.
Indemnification is required regardless of whether the Administrative Agent is a party to the litigation or proceeding.
Indemnification is required regardless of whether the litigation or proceeding is brought by the Borrower, its equity holders, its affiliates, or any other person.
The Administrative Agent is indemnified against any costs arising from its own ordinary negligence.

The Administrative Agent is excluded from indemnification only if a court of competent jurisdiction determines by a final and non-appealable judgment that the losses resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct of the Administrative Agent.
The Administrative Agent is excluded from indemnification if losses result from a material breach of the Credit Agreement by the Administrative Agent.
The Administrative Agent is excluded from indemnification if losses result from a material breach of any other Loan Document by the Administrative Agent.
The Administrative Agent is excluded from indemnification if losses result from a material breach of the Credit Agreement by its Related Parties.
The Administrative Agent is excluded from indemnification if losses result from a material breach of any other Loan Document by its Related Parties.
The indemnification obligation survives the termination of the Credit Agreement.
The indemnification obligation survives the payment of all Loans.
The indemnification obligation survives the resignation or removal of the Administrative Agent.

Section 10.03 is titled 'No Waiver; Cumulative Remedies'.
No failure or delay by any Lender or the Administrative Agent in exercising any right, remedy, power, or privilege under the Credit Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof.
No single or partial exercise of any right, remedy, power, or privilege under the Credit Agreement precludes any other or further exercise thereof.
The exercise of any right, remedy, power, or privilege under the Credit Agreement does not preclude the exercise of any other right, remedy, power, or privilege.
The rights, remedies, powers, and privileges provided in the Credit Agreement are cumulative.
The rights, remedies, powers, and privileges provided in the Credit Agreement are not exclusive of any rights, remedies, powers, or privileges provided by law.
The Administrative Agent is a party subject to the provisions of Section 10.03.
The Lenders are parties subject to the provisions of Section 10.03.
Section 10.04 governs Assignments and Participations.
Lenders may assign all or a portion of their rights and obligations under the Credit Agreement.
Assignments are subject to the prior written consent of the Administrative Agent.
Assignments are subject to the prior written consent of the Borrower.
Borrower consent for assignments is not required if an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing.
Assignments to an Affiliate of a Lender do not require Borrower consent.
Assignments to an Approved Fund do not require Borrower consent.
The minimum assignment amount for Term Loans is $1,000,000.
The minimum assignment amount for Revolving Commitments is $1,000,000.
Assignments must be in increments of $1,000,000 in excess of the minimum amount.
The Administrative Agent shall maintain a register for the recordation of the names and addresses of the Lenders.
The Administrative Agent shall maintain a register for the recordation of the Commitments of each Lender.
The Administrative Agent shall maintain a register for the recordation of the principal amounts of the Loans.
The Administrative Agent shall maintain a register for the recordation of the interest amounts of the Loans.
The entries in the register shall be conclusive, absent manifest error.
Lenders may grant participations in all or a portion of their rights and obligations.
A participant shall not be entitled to require the Lender to take or omit to take any action.
The Lender remains solely responsible for the performance of its obligations under the Credit Agreement.
The Borrower agrees that each participant shall be entitled to the benefits of Sections 2.15, 2.16, and 2.17.
Participants are subject to the limitations of Sections 2.15, 2.16, and 2.17 as if they were a Lender.

entries.content: Section 10.18 is titled 'Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions'.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The provision applies to each party to the credit agreement.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The provision acknowledges that any Affected Financial Institution may be subject to the Write-Down and Conversion Powers of an Applicable Resolution Authority.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The parties agree and consent to the application of any Write-Down and Conversion Powers by an Applicable Resolution Authority to any liability or obligation of an Affected Financial Institution.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The consent includes the reduction of all or a portion of the principal amount or outstanding amount due on any liability.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The consent includes the conversion of all or a portion of the liability into shares, other securities, or other instruments of ownership.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The consent includes the cancellation of the liability.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The consent includes the amendment or alteration of the terms of the liability.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The consent includes the suspension of any payment obligations under the liability.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'Affected Financial Institution' is defined as (a) any EEA Financial Institution or (b) any UK Financial Institution.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'Applicable Resolution Authority' is defined as any Governmental Authority having the ability to exercise any Write-Down and Conversion Powers.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'EEA Financial Institution' is defined as (a) any credit institution or investment firm established in any EEA Member Country which is subject to the supervision of an EEA Resolution Authority.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'EEA Financial Institution' includes any entity established in an EEA Member Country which is a parent of an institution described in clause (a).

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'EEA Financial Institution' includes any financial institution that is a subsidiary of an institution described in clause (a) or (b) and is subject to consolidated supervision with its parent.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'EEA Member Country' refers to any of the member states of the European Union, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'EEA Resolution Authority' refers to any public administrative authority or any person entrusted with public administrative authority of any EEA Member Country (including any delegee) having responsibility for the resolution of any EEA Financial Institution.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'UK Financial Institution' is defined as any BRRD Undertaking (as such term is defined under the PRA Rulebook) or any person falling within IFPRU 11.6 of the FCA Handbook.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'UK Financial Institution' includes any person who is subject to the supervision of the UK Financial Conduct Authority or the UK Prudential Regulation Authority.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'UK Resolution Authority' refers to the Bank of England or any other public administrative authority having responsibility for the resolution of any UK Financial Institution.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: The term 'Write-Down and Conversion Powers' refers to the powers of an Applicable Resolution Authority to write down or convert the liability of an Affected Financial Institution.

source.section: Section 10.18           Acknowledgement and Consent to Bail-In of Affected Financial Institutions

entries.content: Section 11.02 is titled 'Certificates; Other Information'.
The Borrower is required to deliver a Compliance Certificate to the Administrative Agent.
The Compliance Certificate must be signed by a Responsible Officer of the Borrower.
The Compliance Certificate must be delivered concurrently with the delivery of financial statements.
The Compliance Certificate must set forth the information required by Section 11.02(a).
The Compliance Certificate must state whether any Default or Event of Default has occurred.
If a Default or Event of Default has occurred, the Compliance Certificate must describe the nature and period of existence.
The Compliance Certificate must describe the action the Borrower has taken or proposes to take with respect to any Default or Event of Default.
The Compliance Certificate must include calculations in reasonable detail demonstrating compliance with financial covenants.
The Compliance Certificate must include a statement regarding the calculation of the Consolidated Leverage Ratio.
The Compliance Certificate must include a statement regarding the calculation of the Consolidated Interest Coverage Ratio.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in GAAP or in the application thereof.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the fiscal policy of the Borrower.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the application of accounting principles.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the fiscal year of the Borrower.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the fiscal quarter of the Borrower.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the accounting policies of the Borrower.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the reporting practices of the Borrower.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the financial reporting requirements of the Borrower.
The Borrower must provide notice of any change in the tax accounting methods of the Borrower.
Section 11.09 is titled 'Books and Records; Inspection'.
The Borrower is required to keep proper books of record and account.
The Borrower must make full and correct entries in its books of record and account.
The Borrower must maintain books in conformity with GAAP.
The Borrower must maintain books in conformity with all requirements of law.
The Borrower must permit representatives of the Administrative Agent to visit and inspect any of its properties.
The Borrower must permit representatives of the Administrative Agent to examine and make abstracts from any of its books and records.
The Borrower must permit representatives of the Administrative Agent to discuss its affairs, finances, and accounts with its officers.
The Borrower must permit representatives of the Administrative Agent to discuss its affairs, finances, and accounts with its independent public accountants.
Inspections by the Administrative Agent may occur at any reasonable time.
Inspections by the Administrative Agent may occur from time to time.
Inspections by the Administrative Agent may occur as often as may be reasonably desired.
The Borrower must authorize its independent public accountants to discuss its affairs, finances, and accounts with the Administrative Agent.
The Administrative Agent may conduct inspections upon reasonable advance notice.
The Administrative Agent may conduct inspections during normal business hours.
The Borrower is responsible for the costs of inspections conducted during the continuance of an Event of Default.
The Administrative Agent may exercise inspection rights through its agents or employees.
The inspection rights apply to the Borrower and its Subsidiaries.
The Borrower must ensure that its Subsidiaries comply with the inspection and record-keeping requirements.
The Administrative Agent's right to inspect includes the right to make copies of records.
Section 11.10 title is 'Additional Collateral; Additional Guarantors'.
The Borrower is required to cause each Restricted Subsidiary that is a Domestic Subsidiary to become a Guarantor.
The obligation to add a Guarantor applies to any Restricted Subsidiary that is a Domestic Subsidiary.

Section 11.12 is titled Anti-Corruption Compliance.
The Borrower must ensure that no part of the proceeds of the Loans will be used in violation of the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977.
The Borrower must ensure that no part of the proceeds of the Loans will be used in violation of the UK Bribery Act 2010.

entries.content: Schedule 4.01 is titled Closing Date Conditions Checklist.
Item 1: Executed Credit Agreement by Borrower HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC.
Item 2: Executed Credit Agreement by Administrative Agent Northbrook Capital Markets.
Item 3: Executed Credit Agreement by all Lenders.
Item 4: Certified copy of organizational documents for HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC.
Item 5: Good standing certificates for Borrower in jurisdiction of organization.
Item 6: Secretary's Certificate of Borrower attaching resolutions authorizing the Credit Agreement.
Item 7: Incumbency certificate for authorized signatories of Borrower.
Item 8: Legal opinion of counsel to Borrower regarding enforceability.
Item 9: Solvency certificate signed by the Chief Financial Officer of Borrower.
Item 10: Perfection certificate regarding collateral security interests.
Item 11: UCC-1 financing statements filed in appropriate jurisdictions.
Item 12: Evidence of insurance coverage naming Administrative Agent as loss payee.
Item 13: Payment of all fees required by the Commitment Letter.
Item 14: Payment of all expenses required by the Commitment Letter.
Item 15: Receipt of audited financial statements for the most recent fiscal year.
Item 16: Receipt of unaudited interim financial statements for the most recent quarter.
Item 17: Pro forma balance sheet reflecting the Transactions.
Item 18: No Material Adverse Effect has occurred since the date of the Commitment Letter.
Item 19: Accuracy of all representations and warranties in the Credit Agreement.
Item 20: Absence of any Default or Event of Default.
Schedule 5.05 is titled 'Financial Statements'.
The document is identified as Schedule 5.05 of the draft credit agreement.
The schedule pertains to the delivery requirements for financial statements.
The schedule is currently empty/blank in the provided source text.
The absence of content in Schedule 5.05 constitutes a material omission for the financial reporting covenant.
The schedule reference '5.05' implies a corresponding Section 5.05 in the main body of the credit agreement.
The document lacks the required list of historical financial statements for the Borrower.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for the Guarantors.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any Material Subsidiaries.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any Unrestricted Subsidiaries.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any acquired entities.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any pro forma financial information.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any historical audit reports.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any quarterly review reports.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any management discussion and analysis (MD&A).
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any compliance certificates.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any budget projections.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any capital expenditure forecasts.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any liquidity reports.
The document lacks the required list of financial statements for any covenant calculation worksheets.
Schedule 6.01 lists Existing Indebtedness of the Borrower and its Subsidiaries.
Item 1: $150,000,000 aggregate principal amount of 7.50% Senior Secured Notes due 2028 issued by Ridgeline Manufacturing Group LLC.
Item 2: $25,000,000 revolving credit facility provided by First National Bank of Chicago, maturing June 30, 2025.
Item 3: Equipment lease obligation with Caterpillar Financial Services Corporation, monthly payments of $12,500, expiring December 31, 2026.
Item 4: $5,000,000 intercompany loan from Hawthorne Capital Holdings LP to HC Ridgeline Holdings, interest rate 5.00% per annum.
Item 5: $2,000,000 letter of credit issued by Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. for the benefit of the City of Houston, expiring January 15, 2026.
Item 6: $750,000 capital lease obligation with Dell Financial Services, quarterly payments of $50,000, maturing September 30, 2025.
Item 7: $1,200,000 promissory note payable to former shareholders of Ridgeline Manufacturing Group LLC, 4.00% interest, due December 31, 2024.
Item 8: $3,500,000 industrial revenue bond issued by the State of Texas, 3.25% interest, maturing July 1, 2030.
Item 9: $500,000 vehicle financing agreement with Ford Motor Credit Company, 6.50% interest, maturing March 31, 2027.
Item 10: $10,000,000 term loan facility with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., interest rate SOFR + 350 bps, maturing December 31, 2027.
Item 11: $250,000 unsecured loan from Hawthorne Capital Management LLC, non-interest bearing, due on demand.
Item 12: $1,500,000 equipment financing with Siemens Financial Services, Inc., 5.75% interest, maturing June 30, 2026.
Item 13: $800,000 revolving line of credit with PNC Bank, N.A., interest rate Prime + 1.00%, maturing August 15, 2025.
Item 14: $4,000,000 subordinated debt held by Hawthorne Capital Partners, 10.00% PIK interest, maturing January 1, 2032.
Item 15: $600,000 software license financing with Oracle America, Inc., 0% interest, 36 monthly installments ending December 2025.
Item 16: $2,200,000 real estate mortgage with Bank of America, N.A., 4.50% interest, maturing October 31, 2035.
Item 17: $300,000 credit card facility with American Express, variable interest rate, due monthly.
Item 18: $900,000 construction loan with Regions Bank, 5.25% interest, maturing May 1, 2026.
Item 19: $1,100,000 equipment lease with HP Financial Services, 4.75% interest, maturing November 30, 2026.
Item 20: $450,000 working capital loan from Ridgeline Manufacturing Group LLC to HC Ridgeline Holdings, 3.00% interest, due December 31, 2025.
Schedule 6.02 identifies Existing Liens as of the Closing Date.
Item 1: UCC-1 Financing Statement filed by First National Bank against Ridgeline Manufacturing Group, LLC, dated March 12, 2019, File No. 2019-004521.
Item 2: Security Interest in favor of Equipment Leasing Corp. regarding CNC Machinery, recorded June 5, 2020, UCC-1 File No. 2020-88921.
Item 3: Mortgage in favor of Industrial Realty Trust on the manufacturing facility located at 4500 Industrial Way, Chicago, IL, dated January 15, 2018.
Item 4: Lien on intellectual property assets held by TechPatent Holdings, LLC, pursuant to Security Agreement dated November 20, 2021.
Item 5: Statutory landlord lien on the warehouse facility at 1200 Logistics Drive, Memphis, TN, per lease agreement dated August 1, 2017.
Item 6: Purchase Money Security Interest (PMSI) held by HeavyDuty Trucks, Inc. on fleet vehicles, UCC-1 File No. 2022-11034.
Item 7: Tax lien filed by the State of Illinois Department of Revenue against Ridgeline Manufacturing Group, LLC, dated February 10, 2023, for $45,200.
Item 8: Lien on inventory in favor of SupplyChain Finance Partners, LLC, pursuant to Inventory Financing Agreement dated May 14, 2021.
Item 9: Mechanic's lien filed by BuildRight Construction, Inc. regarding facility expansion, dated September 30, 2023, in the amount of $125,000.
Item 10: Security interest in favor of Global Bank N.A. on all deposit accounts, per Control Agreement dated October 5, 2019.
