ABR definition clause (a): Prime Rate
ABR definition clause (b): Federal Funds Rate + 0.50%
ABR definition clause (c): Adjusted Term SOFR (1-month) + 1.00%
ABR floor: 1.00% per annum
Adjusted Daily Simple SOFR lookback: 5 U.S. Government Securities Business Days
Term Loan Applicable Rate (SOFR): 4.25% per annum
Term Loan Applicable Rate (ABR): 3.25% per annum
Revolving Loan Applicable Rate (SOFR): 3.75% per annum
Revolving Loan Applicable Rate (ABR): 2.75% per annum
Borrower registered agent: Continental Registered Agents, Inc., 160 Greentree Drive, Suite 101, Dover, DE 19904
Borrower EIN: 85-4023198
Business Day definition: excludes Saturdays, Sundays, and NYC bank holidays
Cash Equivalents commercial paper rating: A-1 by S&P or P-1 by Moody's
Cash Equivalents commercial paper maturity: 270 days
Cash Equivalents bank capital/surplus requirement: $500,000,000
Change of Control threshold: Hawthorne Capital Partners, LP and Permitted Holders < 50.1% voting power
Closing Date First Lien Net Leverage Ratio: 3.59 to 1.00
Closing Date Total Net Leverage Ratio: 3.85 to 1.00
Commitment Letter date: May 22, 2025
Consolidated EBITDA add-back: interest expense
Consolidated EBITDA add-back: income taxes
Consolidated EBITDA add-back: depreciation and amortization
Consolidated EBITDA add-back: non-cash charges/write-downs
Consolidated EBITDA add-back: transaction costs/fees
Consolidated EBITDA add-back: restructuring/business optimization costs
Benchmark Replacement order: (a) Adjusted Daily Simple SOFR, (b) Administrative Agent/Borrower selected rate
Benchmark Transition Event: public statement by administrator regarding cessation of tenor
Benchmark Transition Event: public statement by regulatory supervisor regarding cessation
Benchmark Transition Event: public statement by regulatory supervisor regarding non-representativeness
ABR definition: Adjusted Term SOFR for 1-month Interest Period + 1.00%
ABR definition: Prime Rate effective date is same as change date
Adjusted Daily Simple SOFR: greater of SOFR + Adjustment or Floor
Adjusted Term SOFR: greater of Term SOFR + Adjustment or Floor
Administrative Agent: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC
Affiliate Control definition: power to direct management/policies
Anti-Corruption Laws: FCPA 1977, UK Bribery Act 2010
Asset Sale definition: excludes ordinary course inventory/obsolete assets
Asset Sale definition: excludes Cash Equivalents
Asset Sale definition: excludes Section 6.03 mergers
Asset Sale definition: excludes Section 6.04/6.05 permitted payments/investments
Audited Financial Statements: 2022, 2023, 2024
Bail-In Legislation: EEA Member Country implementation of Directive 2014/59/EU
Bail-In Legislation: UK Banking Act 2009
Benchmark Replacement Adjustment: spread adjustment based on Relevant Governmental Body recommendation
Benchmark Replacement Conforming Changes: technical/administrative changes by Admin Agent
Benchmark Replacement Date: earlier of cessation statement or non-representativeness announcement
Board of Directors definition: includes board of managers/managing member for LLCs
Capital Expenditures definition: GAAP-based purchase of property/equipment
Capitalized Lease Obligations: excludes ASC 842 operating leases as of Dec 31, 2024
Cash Equivalents: US Treasury obligations maturing within 1 year
Cash Equivalents: repurchase obligations term limit 30 days
CFC Holdco definition: domestic parent of CFC with no material assets other than CFC equity
Company Material Adverse Effect: based on Acquisition Agreement as of May 15, 2025
CME Term SOFR Administrator: CME Group Benchmark Administration Limited
Reference date for measuring effect is December 31, 2024.
Compliance Certificate form is defined as Exhibit A.
Conforming Changes defined as Benchmark Replacement Conforming Changes.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (a): consolidated interest expense.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (b): provision for income taxes.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (c): depreciation and amortization expense.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (d): non-cash charges, write-downs, expenses, or losses (excluding accruals for future cash expenditures).
Consolidated EBITDA addback (e): transaction-related fees, costs, and expenses.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (f): restructuring, business optimization, and integration costs.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (f) cap: greater of $10,000,000 and 10% of Consolidated EBITDA.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (g): projected cost savings/synergies realizable within 12 months.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (g) cap: 20% of Consolidated EBITDA.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (h): management/advisory fees paid to Sponsor per Section 6.07.
Consolidated EBITDA addback (i): other non-cash items approved by Administrative Agent.
Consolidated EBITDA deduction (x): non-cash gains and items increasing Consolidated Net Income.
Consolidated EBITDA deduction (y): extraordinary, unusual, or non-recurring gains.
Consolidated First Lien Debt: aggregate principal amount of Consolidated Total Debt secured on a first-priority basis.
Consolidated Interest Expense: total consolidated interest expense including OID amortization and net Hedge Agreement payments.

Consolidated Net Income exclusions: (a) non-Restricted Subsidiary income, (b) restricted dividend payments, (c) accounting principle changes, (d) non-ordinary course Asset Sale gains/losses, (e) non-cash compensation charges.
Consolidated Total Assets: total assets on a consolidated basis per most recent balance sheet.
Consolidated Total Debt: aggregate principal amount of Indebtedness including Capitalized Lease Obligations, excluding undrawn letters of credit and Hedge Agreements.
Defaulting Lender criteria: (a) failure to fund within 2 Business Days, (b) written notice of non-compliance, (c) failure to confirm funding within 3 Business Days, (d) insolvency or Bail-In Action.
Designated Non-Cash Consideration cap: greater of $10,000,000 and 10% of Consolidated Total Assets.
Equity Contribution: minimum $330,000,000 cash equity from Hawthorne Capital Partners Fund VI, LP.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (b): cash Capital Expenditures not financed by Indebtedness.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (c): net cash interest expense.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (d): scheduled principal payments of Indebtedness.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (e): cash taxes paid.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (f): increase in Working Capital.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (g): Permitted Investments funded with internally generated cash.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (h): voluntary Term Loan prepayments funded with internally generated cash.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (i): cash restructuring/integration costs.
Excess Cash Flow deduction (j): cash Restricted Payments permitted under Section 6.04.
Excluded Assets (a): fee-owned real property < $2,500,000.
Excluded Assets (b): leasehold interests in real property.
Excluded Assets (c): motor vehicles subject to certificates of title.
Excluded Assets (d): governmental licenses/franchises where security interest is prohibited.
Excluded Assets (e): property where security interest violates law or requires unobtained consent.
Excluded Assets (f): intent-to-use trademark applications.
Excluded Subsidiary (a): non-wholly owned Subsidiaries.
Excluded Subsidiary (b): Immaterial Subsidiaries.
Excluded Subsidiary (c): CFCs.
Excluded Subsidiary (d): CFC Holdcos.
Excluded Subsidiary (e): Subsidiaries prohibited by law/contract from guaranteeing.
Excluded Taxes (a): net income, franchise, and branch profits taxes.
Excluded Taxes (b): U.S. federal withholding taxes in effect on date of interest acquisition.
Excluded Taxes (c): taxes from failure to comply with Section 2.12(d).
Excluded Taxes (d): FATCA taxes.
Extraordinary Receipts (a): insurance proceeds (excluding business interruption).
Extraordinary Receipts (b): condemnation awards.
Extraordinary Receipts (c): indemnification payments.
Extraordinary Receipts (d): purchase price adjustments.
Extraordinary Receipts (e): tax refunds.
Extraordinary Receipts (f): pension plan reversions.
Fee Letter date: May 22, 2025.
Fee Letter parties: Borrower and Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC.
First Lien Net Leverage Ratio: (Consolidated First Lien Debt - Unrestricted Cash) / Consolidated EBITDA.

entries.content: Sender: David Sung, Managing Director, Leveraged Finance, Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC
Recipient: Jennifer Whitfield, akplaw.com
CC: Marcus Roth, Hawthorne Capital
Date of email: Mon, 02 Jun 2025 03:47:00 -0000
Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC will not exercise any market flex rights as set forth in the Fee Letter dated May 22, 2025
Fee Letter date: May 22, 2025
Parties to Fee Letter: Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC and HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC
Transaction: Financing of HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC's acquisition of Ridgeline Manufacturing Group, Inc.

Scope of no-flex confirmation: Includes interest rate margins, applicable spread over SOFR, original issue discount (OID), amortization schedules, and any other economic term for Term Loan B or Revolving Credit Facility
Drafting counsel: Everstone Partners LLP (Robert Chiang and team)
Expected delivery date of initial draft credit agreement: Week of June 9, 2025
Commitment Letter and Term Sheet (Exhibit A) date: May 22, 2025
Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC address: 444 West Lake Street, Suite 2600, Chicago, IL 60606
Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC phone: (312) 555-0184
Syndication status: Uniformly positive feedback; market conditions support committed terms
Historical relationship: Three closings together across Funds IV and V
The Term Loan B principal amount is $350,000,000.
The Term Loan B interest rate is Adjusted Term SOFR plus 4.00% (400 basis points) per annum.
The Term Loan B SOFR floor is 0.50% per annum.
The Term Loan B Original Issue Discount is 98.0 (2.0% discount), totaling $7,000,000.
The Term Loan B maturity date is seven (7) years from the Closing Date (expected July 31, 2032).
The Term Loan B amortization is 1.0% per annum, payable in equal quarterly installments of $875,000.
The Revolving Credit Facility commitment amount is $75,000,000.
The Revolving Credit Facility interest rate is Adjusted Term SOFR plus 3.75% (375 basis points) per annum.
The Revolving Credit Facility SOFR floor is 0.00%.
The Revolving Credit Facility commitment fee is 0.375% per annum on the average daily undrawn portion.
The Revolving Credit Facility Letter of Credit sublimit is $15,000,000.
The Revolving Credit Facility Swingline sublimit is $10,000,000.
The Excess Cash Flow sweep percentage is 50% for First Lien Net Leverage Ratio > 3.75x.
The Excess Cash Flow sweep percentage is 25% for First Lien Net Leverage Ratio <= 3.75x but > 3.25x.
The Excess Cash Flow sweep percentage is 0% for First Lien Net Leverage Ratio <= 3.25x.
The Asset Sale annual de minimis threshold is $7,500,000.
The maximum reinvestment period for Asset Sale proceeds is 545 days (365 days initial + 180 days extension).
The Borrower is HC Ridgeline Holdings, LLC.
The Lead Arranger and Administrative Agent is Northbrook Capital Markets, LLC.
The Sponsor is Hawthorne Capital Partners, LP.
FY2024 Adjusted EBITDA is $97,500,000 per the quality of earnings report by Redfield & Associates, Inc.
Incremental Facilities Free-and-Clear Amount is the greater of $75,000,000 or 75% of Consolidated EBITDA.
Closing Date First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is approximately 3.59x based on $350,000,000 Term Loan B and $97,500,000 Adjusted EBITDA.
Closing Date Total Net Leverage Ratio is approximately 3.85x based on $375,000,000 total debt and $97,500,000 Adjusted EBITDA.
MFN Threshold: If effective yield on incremental term loan exceeds existing Term Loan B by more than 50 basis points, existing margin must be increased.
10 basis point differential in SOFR floor is excluded from MFN Threshold calculation.
Cross-default threshold for other material indebtedness is $25,000,000.
Judgment threshold for Event of Default is $25,000,000 individually or in the aggregate.
Minimum assignment amount for Term Loans is $1,000,000.
Minimum assignment amount for Revolving Commitments is $5,000,000.
Assignment fee payable to Administrative Agent is $3,500.
Term Loan B SOFR Margin is 4.00% and ABR Margin is 3.00%.
Revolving Credit Facility SOFR Margin is 3.75% and ABR Margin is 2.75%.
Term Loan B SOFR Floor is 0.50%.
Revolving Credit Facility SOFR Floor is 0.00%.
Default Rate is 2.00% per annum above the otherwise applicable rate.
Targeted Closing Date is July 31, 2025.
Outside Date is September 15, 2025.
Term Loan B Maturity is July 31, 2032.
Revolving Credit Facility Maturity is July 31, 2030.
Row 6.0 identified as 'Term Loan B — Voluntary Prepayment / Soft Call' in comparison-template.xlsx.
